DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENA TO NON-PARTY JONATHAN VASQUEZ. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MR. VASQUEZ STATES THAT HE HAS PRODUCED TO PLAINTIFFS ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS IN HIS POSSESSION, CUSTODY OR CONTROL AND HAS PROVIDED EXTENSIVE EVIDENTIARY MATERIALS IN SUPPORT OF HIS STATEMENT. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT MR. VASQUEZ HAS ADDITIONAL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2026 Continued To Jul-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Renewed Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena To Non-Party Jonathan Vazquez
Off Calendar May-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' RENEWED MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENA TO NON-PARTY JONATHAN VAZQUEZ

Appearances:
Jonathan Vazquez (925.895.8954) opposing party appearing via Zoom in propria persona; No other appearances.

This matter is called at 9:52 am and there is no appearance by plaintiffs, who contest the tentative ruling. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The renewed motion to compel compliance with a subpoena issued to non-party Jonathan Vazquez is denied. The court rejected this relief by written order dated April 21, 2026. Although moving parties present different evidence on this motion, they make no showing why their evidence could not have been presented in the earlier motion, a requirement for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure, section 1008, subdivision (b). (See Shiffer v. CBS Corp. (2015) 240 Cal.App.4th 246, 255 ["the moving party must provide a satisfactory explanation for the failure to produce that evidence at an earlier time."] [citation simplified].) Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena To Non-Party Jonathan Vasquez
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS DERRICK CHEN AND KIET LY'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENA TO NON-PARTY JONATHAN VASQUEZ

Appearances via Zoom:
Derrick Chen (415.218.5728, derrick@cenports.com) defendant and moving party appearing in propria persona;
No other appearances.

Mr. Chen did not give notice of his intent to contest the tentative ruling. The matter is not argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs Derrick Chen and Kiet Ly's motion to compel compliance with subpoena to non-party Jonathan Vasquez is denied. Mr. Vasquez states that he has produced to plaintiffs all responsive documents in his possession, custody or control and has provided extensive evidentiary materials in support of his statement. Plaintiffs have not shown that Mr. Vasquez has additional responsive documents. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
